text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 14 February 2017

 Dr Kate 
 Health Centre 

 Keele University, Keele, Newcastle

 Staffordshire ST5 5BG 
Dear  Dr Kate 
 Wendy Brown , DOB:  21-11-1982 , Hospital No:  K482715  NHS No.  6592841037 
 Flat 7 Brookside Avenue, Newcastle-under-Lyme, ST5 2QD  

Diagnoses/summary: Recurrent seizures under review; current working diagnosis remains under investigation with no definitive classification. No clear structural lesion identified to date. Overall clinical stability improving in recent weeks with better adherence.

Previous investigations: Routine MRI brain (2015) reported as normal. Screening EEG (2015) non-diagnostic. No telemetry performed. Bloods including FBC, U&E, LFTs and bone profile within reference ranges (last checked January 2017).

Medication history: Previously trialled levetiracetam (mood irritability), discontinued. Currently on lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily with good tolerability. Uses vitamin D supplementation. No known drug allergies.

Seizure history and current status: The first seizure was reported in December 2016 while visiting relatives in Canada. It happened during an afternoon nap, with an unusual tingling spreading from the right hand to the shoulder. The second and third event took place in February 2017 in the USA, again from sleep, lasting approximately five minutes, with comparable symptoms. Since introducing a pillbox and timed phone reminders in late January, missed doses have markedly reduced and there has been no further event since early February. No injuries, tongue biting, or incontinence reported. Post-event confusion minimal and resolved within minutes. Partner corroborates the description of events arising from sleep.

Interim progress: The patient reports improved day-to-day consistency, fewer auras, and better sleep hygiene. No recent headaches, visual disturbance, or mood decline. Adherence has improved substantially with the pillbox/reminder system and this correlates with clinical stability.

Examination today: Alert and oriented, normal speech and language. Cranial nerves intact. Motor and coordination normal. No focal neurological deficit. Cardiovascular and respiratory examinations unremarkable. BMI 24. Blood pressure 118/72.

Plan:
- Continue lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily. Reinforced adherence; the pillbox/reminder strategy appears effective and is likely contributing to the current improvement.
- Safety advice reiterated: avoid sleep deprivation, limit alcohol, and follow general seizure first-aid guidance. No driving until standard seizure-free criteria are met per DVLA guidance.
- Arrange repeat EEG to look for interictal changes; consider extended sleep-deprived protocol if routine study is non-diagnostic.
- Check lamotrigine trough level and repeat routine bloods in 6–8 weeks for baseline monitoring.
- Provide written self-management plan and seizure diary template; patient agrees to record any further events with timing, triggers, and recovery details.
- Follow-up in 3 months, earlier if recurrence or concerns.

Overall impression: The clinical picture is improving with better medication adherence and routine. Stability has improved since implementing pillbox and reminders, and there have been no further events reported since early February.

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter describes three seizures: first in December 2016, then two events in February 2017. It also states there have been no further events since early February 2017. The clinic date is 14 February 2017, so the current period covers roughly December to early February (~2 months). Since the patient is not seizure-free for at least 6 months, we must calculate frequency. The highest frequency relevant to the current status would be the recent period with events: 3 events over approximately 2 months (December and early February). Therefore, normalize to '3 per 2 month'.
